text:
WHOLE BODY PET-CT WITH 18F-FLUORODEOXYGLUCOSE (FDG)

EXAMINATION: PET-CT, whole body, 18F-FDG
INDICATION: 66-year-old female with newly diagnosed pancreatic head/uncinate mass on recent pancreatic protocol CT, measuring 3.4 cm with involvement of the superior mesenteric artery and superior mesenteric vein. Presenting with 3-month history of epigastric pain, significant weight loss, new-onset worsening hyperglycemia, and markedly elevated CA 19-9 (1,200 U/mL). Study performed for oncologic staging and evaluation for distant metastatic disease prior to treatment planning.

RADIOPHARMACEUTICAL: 18F-FDG, 10.2 mCi administered intravenously. Blood glucose at time of injection: 186 mg/dL (patient has known type 2 diabetes; glucose elevated above ideal range; imaging proceeding per departmental protocol for oncologic indication with documentation of suboptimal glucose control). Uptake time: 62 minutes.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body PET-CT acquisition from the vertex of the skull to the mid-thigh. Attenuation correction CT performed without intravenous contrast (low-dose technique, 120 kV, auto-mA). PET images reconstructed using time-of-flight iterative algorithm with attenuation and scatter correction. Fused PET/CT images reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes on dedicated workstation. Standard uptake values (SUV) calculated using body weight normalization.

COMPARISON: Pancreatic protocol CT abdomen/pelvis (recent, same institution).

---

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
No abnormal foci of FDG uptake identified in the brain, orbits, salivary glands, or neck. Physiologic activity seen in the vocal cords and tonsillar tissue. No cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular lymph nodes demonstrate pathologic FDG avidity. Thyroid gland shows no focal uptake. No osseous lesions in the calvarium or cervical spine.

CHEST:
Lungs: No pulmonary nodules or masses demonstrate FDG avidity. No pleural effusions with associated metabolic activity. Background pulmonary parenchymal activity within normal physiologic limits.
Mediastinum and hila: No mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes demonstrate increased FDG uptake above background. The mediastinal blood pool activity appears within normal limits. No anterior, middle, or posterior mediastinal masses identified.
Cardiac: Physiologic myocardial FDG uptake, variable and within expected range. No pericardial effusion with increased activity.
Chest wall and bony thorax: No focal bone lesions with abnormal uptake in the ribs, sternum, clavicles, scapulae, or thoracic spine.

ABDOMEN:
Pancreas and Primary Tumor:
There is a focus of markedly intense FDG uptake in the pancreatic head and uncinate process region, corresponding directly to the hypoattenuating mass identified on the recent pancreatic protocol CT. The metabolic activity is conspicuously elevated, with a peak standardized uptake value (SUVmax) of 9.2, well above the background pancreatic parenchymal activity (SUVmean pancreatic body approximately 1.8). The lesion demonstrates heterogeneous internal uptake with a central zone of relatively lower activity, potentially reflecting areas of necrosis or fibrosis within the tumor. The margins of the FDG-avid zone correspond to the poorly defined soft tissue abnormality seen on the cross-sectional component of this study. No satellite foci of FDG avidity are identified in the peripancreatic fat or immediately adjacent tissues to suggest direct extension beyond the primary mass beyond what was characterized on dedicated CT.

Liver:
The liver is normal in size and demonstrates homogeneous background hepatic FDG uptake. No discrete hepatic lesions demonstrate focally increased metabolic activity above hepatic parenchymal background. No wedge-shaped or capsular-based areas of elevated uptake to suggest infiltrative disease. Correlation with the CT component confirms no hepatic hypodensities or masses. The evaluation of hepatic metastases is limited somewhat by the elevated background blood glucose level, which may reduce the sensitivity for detecting small lesions; however, no suspicious foci are identified.

Biliary System and Gallbladder:
No abnormal FDG uptake in the biliary tract or gallbladder. Mild prominence of the common bile duct noted on the attenuation correction CT images in the region of the pancreatic head mass, consistent with early extrinsic compression by the known mass; no hypermetabolic activity along the ductal wall to suggest separate biliary involvement.

Spleen:
The spleen is mildly enlarged at approximately 13.1 cm in craniocaudal dimension on the CT component. FDG uptake in the spleen is mildly elevated, likely reflecting reactive splenomegaly in the setting of systemic malignancy; no focal splenic lesions with discrete increased metabolic activity are identified.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands demonstrate normal background activity without focal hypermetabolic lesions. No adrenal masses identified on the CT component.

Kidneys and Ureters:
Physiologic urinary tract activity with FDG concentration in the renal collecting systems and ureters. No renal parenchymal lesions with focal FDG uptake. No hydronephrosis on the CT component.

Stomach and Bowel:
Background and physiologic luminal bowel activity throughout. No focal areas of increased metabolic activity in the gastric wall, small bowel, or colon beyond physiologic background to suggest additional primary or secondary malignancy. No bowel obstruction on the CT component.

PERIPANCREATIC AND REGIONAL LYMPH NODES:
Multiple small lymph nodes are visible in the peripancreatic, periportal, and perigastric regions on the CT component, the largest measuring up to 9 mm in short axis, consistent with the prior dedicated CT characterization. Importantly, none of these nodes demonstrate FDG avidity above background blood pool activity. No node in the celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, hepatoduodenal ligament, retroperitoneal (para-aortic, aortocaval, or paracaval), or pelvic chains shows pathologic metabolic activity. The absence of FDG-avid nodal disease is reassuring against regional nodal spread, though the metabolic sensitivity for detecting micrometastatic or sub-centimeter nodal disease in the setting of a hyperglycemic study should be acknowledged.

PERITONEUM AND MESENTERY:
No focal peritoneal implants or mesenteric deposits with increased FDG uptake identified. The omentum is unremarkable metabolically. No ascites with associated inflammatory or metabolic activity.

VASCULAR STRUCTURES:
No FDG avidity along the superior mesenteric artery, superior mesenteric vein, portal vein, celiac axis, or aorta to suggest vascular wall infiltration. The relationship of the primary tumor to adjacent vascular structures is better characterized on the dedicated pancreatic protocol CT.

PELVIS:
Uterus demonstrates physiologic background activity appropriate for age. Ovaries are not distinctly visualized. Urinary bladder shows expected intense physiologic FDG activity from urinary excretion. No pelvic lymph nodes demonstrate abnormal FDG avidity. No osseous pelvic lesions.

SKELETAL SURVEY:
No FDG-avid osseous lesions identified throughout the axial or appendicular skeleton to suggest skeletal metastatic disease. No lytic or sclerotic bone lesions on the CT component. Mild degenerative changes in the lumbar spine without associated metabolic activity.

SOFT TISSUES:
No abnormal FDG uptake in the chest wall, abdominal wall, or subcutaneous tissues. No skin lesions with metabolic activity.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. INTENSELY HYPERMETABOLIC MASS IN THE PANCREATIC HEAD AND UNCINATE PROCESS (SUVmax 9.2), corresponding to the known primary tumor. The degree of FDG avidity is markedly elevated and consistent with aggressive, metabolically active pancreatic malignancy. No satellite foci of uptake beyond the primary mass.

2. NO FDG-AVID HEPATIC LESIONS. The liver is metabolically unremarkable. No hepatic metastatic disease identified on this examination.

3. NO FDG-AVID PERITONEAL OR OMENTAL DEPOSITS. No metabolic evidence of peritoneal carcinomatosis.

4. NO PATHOLOGICALLY FDG-AVID LYMPH NODES in the peripancreatic, celiac, mesenteric, retroperitoneal, or pelvic nodal chains. Regional lymph nodes seen on CT are small and metabolically silent.

5. NO EVIDENCE OF DISTANT METASTATIC DISEASE in the chest, thoracic skeleton, or pelvis.

6. MILD REACTIVE SPLENOMEGALY without focal FDG-avid lesions.

OVERALL IMPRESSION: This whole-body FDG PET-CT demonstrates a solitary site of markedly elevated metabolic activity confined to the primary pancreatic head/uncinate mass, without detectable regional nodal or distant metastatic spread. The findings are consistent with locoregionally confined disease. Importantly, the study was performed in the setting of suboptimal glycemic control (blood glucose 186 mg/dL at injection), which may modestly reduce the sensitivity for detecting small-volume or low-grade FDG-avid lesions; this limitation is acknowledged. Clinical and multidisciplinary correlation with the recent dedicated pancreatic protocol CT is recommended for comprehensive local staging and surgical/treatment planning.
